name: tasks
title: Tasks
description: Work items with an enforced status lifecycle. Transition guards add who-per-verb on top of row-filtered update rights. v6 adds task_type/template_id/instructions/metadata (the 'Work / CreateWork' fields reconciled against a private predecessor-system audit, not part of this repo); status/urgency and every transition guard are unchanged.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.help: How-to guidance for whoever works this task, distinct from description -- the audited predecessor's create_task keeps the two separate.

fields.name: template_id
fields.label: Template
fields.help: Optional link to the app-templates record this task was created from or is filling out -- the Work/CreateWork link. Seeding a new task's fields from the template's schema/default_values on create is not built here (see app-tasks/objects/site/tasks.py); this field just carries the reference.

fields.name: metadata
fields.label: Metadata (JSON)
fields.help: Optional free-form structured data. Stored as a JSON string, same convention as app-templates' schema/default_values fields (this codebase's field-type contract, docs/schema-forms.md, has no dedicated json type).

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.help: approved and cancelled are terminal. draft moves and cancelling require the task owner; returning or submitting an assigned task requires the current assignee; resolving waiting_on_client or a dispute requires the owner. Anyone with update rights may accept an open task.
